sn47.35:3.2: This is the closest thing in the Suttas to the modern meditative practices known as “bare awareness”. Here, this meditative practice is said to be the “awareness” part of “mindfulness and awareness” (satisampajañña), as an complement to situational awareness in daily activities (SN 47.2). At DN 33:1.11.37 = AN 4.41:4.1 it is a further development of immersion leading to mindfulness and awareness, at AN 7.38:1.6 and AN 7.39:1.6 it leads to the four kinds of textual analysis, and at MN 123:22.2 it is one of the marvelous qualities of the Buddha.
